title: Hello Jules,
content:
Hello Jules,
I was pleased with the cleanup and organization of the .github repository. I would like you to apply the exact same process to the [repository name here] repository.
The goals for this repository are the same:
  1. Merge all branches: Consolidate all feature branches into the main branch, deleting them after a successful merge.
  2. Resolve conflicts: When conflicts arise, favor the newer or more complete code.
  3. Clean up pull requests: Review all open pull requests and propose a course of action (merge or close) for my approval.
  4. Organize documentation: If one doesn't exist, create a docs/ directory and move all relevant documentation files (like guides, contribution policies, etc.) into it to centralize our knowledge base.
  5. Update README: Modify the main README.md to reflect the new documentation structure and ensure all links are correct.
Please begin with your deep planning mode to ask any clarifying questions about the [repository name here] repository before you create a plan. Our North Star remains to establish best practices, as this repository is also a rough draft.
